questions.prompt: Market prices moved up and down because investors were uncertain.

feedback.betterVersion: Prices fluctuated due to uncertainty.
feedback.why: Fluctuated is stronger than changed because it shows unstable movement.

questions.prompt: Drag to match each term with its meaning.

feedback.betterVersion: Prices can soar, plummet, plateau, or fluctuate depending on sentiment.
feedback.why: Using exact verbs improves analytical accuracy.

feedback.betterVersion: Prices fluctuated due to uncertainty.
feedback.why: Professional language is specific and evidence-based.

feedback.betterVersion: Prices fluctuated due to market uncertainty.
feedback.why: Clear cause-and-effect structure makes your sentence professional.

questions.prompt: A central bank unexpectedly raises interest rates. Which line sounds like market analysis?
options: Prices fluctuated after the rate hike.
questions.answer: Prices fluctuated after the rate hike.

feedback.betterVersion: Prices fluctuated after the rate hike.
feedback.why: It links market behavior to a concrete macro trigger.

feedback.betterVersion: Market makers support liquidity while institutions move large volumes.
feedback.why: Correct role labels avoid vague explanations.

questions.prompt: Stock prices are rising and investors are optimistic.

feedback.betterVersion: This is a bull market.
feedback.why: Bull market is the exact market-state term.

questions.prompt: A lot of individual investors entered the market.

feedback.betterVersion: Retail investors entered the market.
feedback.why: It uses the proper participant category.

feedback.betterVersion: Institutional investors increased their exposure to emerging markets.
feedback.why: Exposure is the correct investment action term.

feedback.betterVersion: Institutional investors increased exposure.
feedback.why: The strongest line is specific and technical.

questions.prompt: Inflation rose, ___ the central bank raised interest rates.

feedback.betterVersion: Inflation rose; consequently, the central bank raised rates.
feedback.why: Consequently correctly marks cause and effect.

questions.prompt: Match each term to its meaning.

feedback.betterVersion: GDP slowed while inflation stayed elevated.
feedback.why: Correct definitions improve analysis quality.

options: Inflation rose, triggering concerns about rate hikes.
questions.answer: Inflation rose, triggering concerns about rate hikes.

feedback.betterVersion: Inflation rose, triggering concerns about rate hikes.
feedback.why: It includes both event and implication.

feedback.betterVersion: Inflation rose due to stronger consumer demand.
feedback.why: Cause phrase placement is essential in analysis writing.

questions.prompt: GDP growth slowed and inflation remained high. Pick the best analysis sentence.
options: The data suggests weaker growth and persistent inflation.
questions.answer: The data suggests weaker growth and persistent inflation.

feedback.betterVersion: The data suggests weaker growth and persistent inflation.
feedback.why: Evidence-led phrasing sounds professional and objective.

modules.name: Performance
modules.title: Module 02｜Performance｜財報
modules.subtitle: Corporate finance, IB, and wealth management

questions.prompt: The company made more money after costs.

feedback.betterVersion: The company improved profitability.
feedback.why: Profitability is a finance-specific metric.

pairs.meaning: earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization

feedback.betterVersion: Despite revenue growth, margins remained razor-thin.
feedback.why: Accurate margin terms reduce ambiguity.

questions.prompt: The company has very small margins.

feedback.betterVersion: The company operates on razor-thin margins.
feedback.why: Razor-thin is an accepted business expression.

feedback.betterVersion: The company improved its net margin despite weaker demand.
feedback.why: It balances performance and headwind in one line.

feedback.betterVersion: The company improved profitability.
feedback.why: Professional reporting should reference measurable outcomes.

pairs.meaning: arranging and guaranteeing a financial deal

feedback.betterVersion: The IPO valuation reflects expected growth.
feedback.why: Correct labels make deal communication clearer.

questions.prompt: A company wants to list its shares publicly.
options: The company wants to be famous.

feedback.betterVersion: The company plans to launch an IPO.
feedback.why: IPO is the standard market term.

questions.prompt: The company's price is too high.
options: The company's valuation is too high.
questions.answer: The company's valuation is too high.

feedback.betterVersion: The company's valuation appears stretched.
feedback.why: Valuation is the technical framing.

feedback.betterVersion: The deal may affect key stakeholders.
feedback.why: This balances caution and impact.

questions.prompt: A company is being acquired by a larger competitor.
options: The company is involved in an M&A deal.
The company has a business thing.
questions.answer: The company is involved in an M&A deal.

feedback.betterVersion: The company is involved in an M&A deal.
feedback.why: M&A language is concise and professional.

feedback.betterVersion: We revised asset allocation to strengthen the portfolio.
feedback.why: Accurate terms improve client clarity.

questions.prompt: This fund did better than the market.

feedback.betterVersion: This fund outperformed the market.
feedback.why: Outperformed is the standard benchmark verb.

questions.prompt: Fund A return: 8%. Fund B return: 5%.
options: Fund A is greater than Fund B.
Fund A is happier than Fund B.

feedback.betterVersion: Fund A outperformed Fund B.
feedback.why: It states comparative performance correctly.

feedback.betterVersion: We adjusted the portfolio to improve asset allocation.
feedback.why: It shows action and purpose in one professional line.

items: This fund did better than the market.
order: This fund did better than the market.

feedback.betterVersion: This fund outperformed the market.
feedback.why: Benchmark language sounds more credible.

questions.prompt: Client is worried about market volatility. Choose the softest recommendation.
options: We could look at rebalancing part of your portfolio.
questions.answer: We could look at rebalancing part of your portfolio.

feedback.betterVersion: We could look at rebalancing part of your portfolio.
feedback.why: Could sounds collaborative and lowers pressure.

questions.prompt: Set the slider to the soft end for client-friendly tone.

feedback.betterVersion: We might consider rebalancing based on your goals.
feedback.why: Softer tone improves trust in sensitive discussions.

questions.prompt: By rebalancing now, we would be locking in your profits. What does 'would' do here?

feedback.betterVersion: By rebalancing now, we would lock in gains.
feedback.why: Would frames a likely benefit without sounding absolute.

questions.original: You must move your capital into fixed income.
replacements: We could look at moving some capital into fixed income.
questions.answer: We could look at moving some capital into fixed income.

feedback.betterVersion: We could look at moving some capital into fixed income.
feedback.why: Suggestion language sounds more consultative.

questions.prompt: You want to suggest opportunity but avoid overconfidence.
options: There might be an opportunity to enter the market next month.
questions.answer: There might be an opportunity to enter the market next month.

feedback.betterVersion: There might be an opportunity to enter next month.
feedback.why: It stays prudent while still suggesting action.

pairs.meaning: something that changes the situation significantly
how a product is placed in the market

feedback.betterVersion: Our positioning clarifies the USP in a crowded market.
feedback.why: Correct concept pairing sharpens strategy language.

questions.prompt: This product is very different and important.
options: This product is a big thing.
This product is very special stuff.

feedback.betterVersion: This product is a game-changer.
feedback.why: It is concise and business-natural.

questions.prompt: You want the team to focus on overall strategy.
options: Let's look at the big picture.
Let's look at the large photo.
questions.answer: Let's look at the big picture.

feedback.betterVersion: Let's look at the big picture.
feedback.why: It is the exact phrase used in strategy meetings.

feedback.betterVersion: Our USP is strong customer support in a saturated market.
feedback.why: It links USP to market context.

items: Our positioning gives us a competitive advantage.
order: Our positioning gives us a competitive advantage.

feedback.betterVersion: Our positioning gives us a competitive advantage.
feedback.why: The strongest statement ties strategy to outcome.

options: The proposal was approved by the team.

feedback.betterVersion: The team approved the proposal.
feedback.why: Active voice sounds direct and accountable.

options: It is thought that we should review the plan.

feedback.betterVersion: I recommend reviewing the plan.
feedback.why: It shows ownership and decision intent.

questions.prompt: Replace with a clearer, more direct version.
questions.original: Maybe the project can be considered.

feedback.betterVersion: We should prioritize this project.
feedback.why: Direct recommendation improves leadership tone.

feedback.betterVersion: We recommend prioritizing the higher-margin product line.
feedback.why: This wording is direct and executive-ready.

feedback.betterVersion: We recommend prioritizing this option.
feedback.why: Stronger verbs communicate leadership confidence.

feedback.betterVersion: Institutions must comply with mandatory regulations.
feedback.why: Compliance wording should be explicit and formal.

questions.prompt: Banks need to follow the rules.

feedback.betterVersion: Banks must comply with regulations.
feedback.why: This is clear, formal, and policy-appropriate.

feedback.betterVersion: Mandatory means required.
feedback.why: In compliance, mandatory removes ambiguity.

feedback.betterVersion: Financial institutions are required to comply with Basel regulations.
feedback.why: Formal structure fits regulatory communication.

questions.prompt: You need to show this is regulation, not suggestion.
options: This is mandatory under current regulations.
questions.answer: This is mandatory under current regulations.

feedback.betterVersion: This is mandatory under current regulations.
feedback.why: Mandatory clearly signals legal obligation.

feedback.betterVersion: The team flagged activity and looked into the account.
feedback.why: Correct terminology helps procedural accuracy.

feedback.betterVersion: We should flag the transaction.
feedback.why: Flag is the standard escalation action.

questions.prompt: We need to investigate this account.
options: We need to look into this account.
We need to see this account.
We need to watch this account.
We need to look this account.
questions.answer: We need to look into this account.

feedback.betterVersion: We need to look into this account.
feedback.why: Look into is natural and precise in compliance contexts.

feedback.betterVersion: The compliance team flagged the suspicious transaction.
feedback.why: Clear actor + action improves reporting.

questions.prompt: Sort the AML/KYC workflow in order.

feedback.betterVersion: Verify identity -> Flag -> Look into -> Report if necessary.
feedback.why: Process order is critical for AML/KYC control.

feedback.betterVersion: A data breach means unauthorized access to client data.
feedback.why: Precise security language reduces confusion.

questions.prompt: Someone accessed client data without permission.
options: There was unauthorized access to client data.
questions.answer: There was unauthorized access to client data.

feedback.betterVersion: There was unauthorized access to client data.
feedback.why: Unauthorized access is formal and audit-friendly.

feedback.betterVersion: If client data is exposed, then the company must report the breach.
feedback.why: Conditional structure is key in incident response language.

questions.prompt: A system weakness may expose client information.

feedback.betterVersion: This creates a cybersecurity risk.
feedback.why: It names the risk clearly for decision-making.

items: There may be a data issue.
The system may expose client data, creating a cybersecurity risk.
order: There may be a data issue.
The system may expose client data, creating a cybersecurity risk.

feedback.betterVersion: The system may expose client data, creating a cybersecurity risk.
feedback.why: The strongest line identifies cause and risk impact.
